error_pedagogy.stderr_excerpt: unrecognized arguments for 'caption search': --liimt 5; did you mean --limit?

intent_inference.note: difflib.get_close_matches cutoff 0.6 produces levenshtein did-you-mean; falls back to listing valid flags

safety_with_recovery.note: n/a — does not gate a destructive op

determinism_and_reproducibility.note: n/a — error text does not affect determinism

self_documentation.note: when no close match, error lists all valid flags

regression_resistance.note: pins did-you-mean hint + subcommand-scoped usage + exit 2

notes: Unknown-flag error is excellent: subcommand-scoped usage, names the offending flag, and gives a levenshtein 'did you mean --limit?' hint (or lists valid flags on no match). stderr-only, exit 2, pinned by R-001. Only gap: plain text, not JSON.

error_pedagogy.stderr_excerpt: invalid choice: 'not_a_command' (choose from doctor, capabilities, robot-docs, token, search, ...)

intent_inference.note: the difflib did-you-mean path handles unknown FLAGS only; unknown SUBCOMMANDS get argparse's choice list with no typo suggestion

safety_with_recovery.note: n/a — does not gate a destructive op

determinism_and_reproducibility.note: n/a — error text does not affect determinism

regression_resistance.note: asserts unknown command → exit 2

notes: Unknown-subcommand error lists all valid commands (agent can recover) and exits 2 on stderr, pinned by R-002. Gap vs flags: no levenshtein 'did you mean' for mistyped subcommands, so intent_inference is low.

error_pedagogy.stderr_excerpt: doctor: probe 'core' failed: Missing Caption API URL. Set CAPTION_API_URL

intent_inference.note: each failed probe reason names the missing config the operator must supply

safety_with_recovery.note: n/a — read-side probe; no destructive op

determinism_and_reproducibility.note: n/a — probe results deterministic offline

self_documentation.note: doctor notes describe the {organization, features, probes} JSON result

composability.note: failed-probe reasons go to stderr; structured result to stdout; --strict flips exit code

regression_resistance.note: pins never-silent-fail: reasons on stderr, json result, --strict non-zero

notes: The former 'doctor silent failure' surface is fixed: failed probes now print 'doctor: probe X failed: <reason>' to stderr, --output json emits a full {organization, features, probes[]} structure, and --strict exits 1. Pinned by R-004. Not silent anymore.

intent_inference.note: bare/no-args gracefully resolves to full help rather than erroring

safety_with_recovery.note: n/a — read-side; prints help only, no irreversible op

self_documentation.stdout_excerpt: full cheat sheet: per-command usage/notes/examples + exit codes + env vars + agent pointers

regression_resistance.note: pins bare caption → exit 0 with Command Cheat Sheet + Agent quick start

notes: Bare `caption` prints the full agent-oriented help (Agent quick start, env vars, exit codes, per-command cheat sheet) to stdout and exits 0 — a strong first-try outcome that points at capabilities/robot-docs. Pinned by R-006. It emits prose rather than JSON (by design; capabilities is the machine surface), which caps output_parseability.
